# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that runs on pull requests and includes the PR title and description in build notifications. Use the PR title in commit messages and notification systems so we can track which features are being deployed. This helps with our release notes automation.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Extract PR Info for Release Tracking
on:
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, synchronize, reopened]

jobs:
  get_pr_details:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      pr_title: ${{ github.event.pull_request.title }}
      pr_description: ${{ github.event.pull_request.body }}
    steps:
      - name: Display PR Title and Description
        run: |
          echo "--- PR Details ---"
          echo "Title: ${{ github.event.pull_request.title }}"
          echo "Description:"
          echo "${{ github.event.pull_request.body }}"
          echo "------------------"
          echo "These details are also available as job outputs for downstream use."

  # Example of how to use the outputs in another job (e.g., for a notification system or release tag)
  # This job will only run if 'get_pr_details' completes successfully.
  # This job is purely for demonstration and can be removed or replaced with actual notification/release logic.
  use_pr_details:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: get_pr_details
    steps:
      - name: Use PR Title for a Simulated Notification/Commit Message
        run: |
          echo "Simulating a notification or commit message for feature tracking:"
          echo "Feature deployment: '${{ needs.get_pr_details.outputs.pr_title }}'"
          echo "Detailed information for release notes:"
          echo "${{ needs.get_pr_details.outputs.pr_description }}"
